acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance
- Snapshot and manifest types use typed shard paths; invalid paths cannot be constructed.
- build_snapshot/build_snapshot_from_state reject dirty_shards outside of shard set.
- import_checkpoint rejects duplicate or out-of-order entries in state/tombstone/dep/note shards.
- Tests cover path validation, dirty_shards subset enforcement, and duplicate-entry rejection.
description:
Problem
- CheckpointSnapshot/CheckpointManifest store shard paths as raw String and dirty_shards as BTreeSet<String>.
- Path validation is scattered (layout::parse_shard_path, import::validate_rel_path, publish tree builder).
- Shard contents accept duplicates/unsorted lines; insert_live silently overwrites duplicates during import.

Impact
- A typo in dirty_shards can delete or skip unrelated files during incremental export.
- Malformed shards can silently drop data (last write wins) instead of failing fast.
- Violates parse-don't-validate and Scatter (path invariants live in multiple places).
design:
Design
- Introduce validated newtypes: ShardName ("[0-9a-f]{2}.jsonl"), CheckpointShardPath { namespace, kind, shard: ShardName }.
- Use CheckpointShardPath (not String) in CheckpointSnapshot.shards and dirty_shards; derive the string path only at the IO boundary.
- Change CheckpointManifest.files to BTreeMap<CheckpointShardPath, ManifestFile> (or a thin CheckpointFilePath newtype).
- Enforce dirty_shards ⊆ shards at construction (return error if not).
- In import_checkpoint, add canonical validation for shard content:
  - state shards: strictly increasing BeadId, no duplicates.
  - tombstones: strictly increasing BeadId (or TombstoneKey) with no duplicates.
  - deps: strictly increasing DepKey, no duplicates.
  - notes: strictly increasing (bead_id, note_id) with no duplicates.
- Keep ordering guarantees in export (already sorted) and assert in tests.

Scatter fit
- Centralize path parsing/validation in one type; callers only handle typed paths.
- Canonical ordering checks live in one parser per shard kind.

Files
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/layout.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/types.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/manifest.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/export.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/import.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/publish.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/cache.rs

notes.content: Reminder: rendering should stay in command files; avoid moving rendering into shared helpers.

notes.content: Reminder: rendering should stay in command files; avoid moving rendering into shared helpers.

title: checkpoint shards: type the paths and enforce canonical order

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CI exercises migration against an exact v0.1.26-produced store artifact
- [ ] The smoke test verifies note preservation plus strict-load success after migration
- [ ] Fixture generation is documented and reproducible
closed_reason: Added a checked-in v0.1.26 fixture, reproducible generator script, and explicit CI migration compatibility smoke job.
description: We now have source-locked fixture coverage for migrating exact v0.1.26-style store commits, but we still do not exercise a real old-release-produced store artifact in CI. Local attempts to build the old v0.1.26 binary on macOS were blocked by machine-specific linker contamination, so release-binary compatibility is not yet proven hermetically.
design: Check in or generate a small canonical v0.1.26 store artifact from the historical tag in a hermetic environment, then add a CI smoke test that runs current bd migrate detect and bd migrate to 1 against it and verifies notes, deps, and labels survive. Prefer a reproducible fixture-generation script over ad hoc manual artifacts.
title: Add hermetic v0.1.26 store migration smoke in CI
